This was added through to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} Sakamoto Kofun Cluster [[File:Sakamoto Kofun Group (Meiwa), gaikan-1.jpg|thumb|300px|right|{{center|Overview of the Sakamoto Kofun Group}}]] {{location map|Japan Mie|lat_deg=34|lat_min=33|lat_sec=1.70|lon_deg=136|lon_min=37|lon_sec=5.35|map_dot_label=Sakamoto Kofun Group|position=top|width=200|mark=Red pog.svg|marksize=10|caption={{center|location of the Sakamoto Kofun Group}}}} '''Sakamoto Kofun Group''' (坂本古墳群) is a cluster of Kofuns ({{ill|Kofun|en|Kofun|WD=Q1141225|lt=Kofun}}), specifically a {{ill|gunshūfun|ja|群集墳|1=gunshūfun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/gunshūfun|13=User:Immanuelle/Gunshufun|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11609514}}, located in {{ill|Meiwa, Mie|ja|明和町|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Meiwa, Mie|1=Meiwa, Mie|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11240358}}, Sakamoto, Mie Prefecture, Japan. Six of these mounds are Historic Site1 as Mie Prefecture Historic Sites, and the gold-plated bronze sword pommel from Mound 1 has been Historic Site1 a Tangible Cultural Property of Mie Prefecture. == Overview == [[File:Sakamoto Kofun Group (Meiwa), gaikan-2.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|View of Mounds 2–8 from Mound 1}}]] The Sakamoto Kofun Group was constructed on the western edge of a terrace on the {{ill|alluvial terrace|ja|洪積台地|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/alluvial terrace|qq=}} between the Kushida River and Miyagawa River in central Mie Prefecture. Historically, the site was referred to as "Sakamoto Hyakuhachizuka" (Sakamoto’s 108 Mounds) and included over 150 Kofuns of various types, including one {{ill|Zenpō-kōhō-fun|ja|前方後方墳|lt=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|1=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=zenpō-kōhō-fun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Zenpō-kōhō-fun|13=User:Immanuelle/zenpō-kōhō-fun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} (Mound 1), a suspected {{ill|Zenpokoenfun|en|Zenpokoenfun|WD=Q11268718|lt=Zenpokoenfun}} (Mound 5), and other round and square mounds. However, most have since been destroyed, with only six mounds (Mounds 1–6) remaining today. Investigations since 1995 have confirmed the presence of more than 30 buried mounds in addition to those still visible. The most significant is Mound 1, the only zenpō-kōhō-fun in the group and notable for being the last known Kofun of this type constructed in Japan. This mound also stands out for its burial method—a log-shaped wooden coffin (waritakegata mokkan)—and the discovery of a gold-plated bronze sword pommel. Mounds 2 and 3 are square mounds, with Mound 2 yielding earrings and Mound 3 producing iron swords during excavations. The Sakamoto Kofun Group is estimated to have been constructed during the late Kofun period, specifically in the early to mid-7th century. As one of the largest Kofun clusters in Mie Prefecture at the time, it is closely associated with the development of the Saikū (斎宮) system approximately 1 kilometer south of the site in 674 AD. This connection adds to the historical importance of the site. The six remaining mounds (Mounds 1–4, 6, and 8) were Historic Site1 as a Historic Site by Mie Prefecture in 2004. The site has been developed into the Sakamoto Kofun Park for public access. == Mound 1 == {{Infobox ancient site | name = Sakamoto Mound 1 | image =[[File:Sakamoto Kofun Group (Meiwa)-1, funkyu.jpg|280px]]
Restored mound (rear section on the left, front section on the right); File:Sakamoto Kofun Group (Meiwa), gaikan-2.jpg| location ={{ill|Meiwa, Mie|ja|明和町|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Meiwa, Mie|1=Meiwa, Mie|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11240358}}, Sakamoto (within Sakamoto Kofun Park); {{ill|Meiwa|1=Meiwa|2=ja|3=明和|WD=Q1203258|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1146161}}| coordinates ={{coordinates|34|33|1.70|N|136|37|5.35|E}}; {{Coord|34.550472222222226|N|136.61815277777777|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}}| type = {{ill|Zenpō-kōhō-fun|ja|前方後方墳|lt=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|1=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=zenpō-kōhō-fun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Zenpō-kōhō-fun|13=User:Immanuelle/zenpō-kōhō-fun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} | length = 38.0 meters | height = 4.2 meters (rear section) | burial = Waritakegata mokkan (log-shaped wooden coffin) | burial goods = Iron swords, {{ill|Sue pottery|en|Sue pottery|WD=Q7634351|lt=Sue pottery}} | built = Early 7th century | notes = Last known zenpō-kōhō-fun }} Mound 1 is the primary Kofun of the Sakamoto Kofun Group. Excavations were conducted on the mound and surrounding moat in 1996, and its burial chamber was examined in 1997. The zenpō-kōhō-fun mound faces south-southwest and has the following area: * Total length: 38.0 meters * Rear section: ** Length: 20.2 meters ** Width: 17.8 meters ** Height: 4.2 meters * Front section: ** Length: 11.0 meters ** Width: 15.0 meters ** Height: 2.6 meters A surrounding moat measures 2.5–4.6 meters wide and 0.6–1.0 meters deep. The burial chamber in the center of the rear section contained a log-shaped wooden coffin measuring 4.72 meters long, 0.74 meters wide, and 0.44 meters high, placed in a pit measuring 6.5 meters long, 3.5 meters wide, and 0.5–0.8 meters deep. burial goods recovered from the chamber include: * Gold-plated bronze sword pommel * Iron swords (2) * Sue pottery (yokobingata, heibingata, and hibashi types) The mound's construction is dated to the early 7th century, making it the last known zenpō-kōhō-fun in Japan. The use of a log-shaped wooden coffin and the discovery of the gold-plated bronze sword pommel, the first of its kind in Mie Prefecture, highlight its significance. 坂本1号墳 (明和町) 出土 直刀.JPG|Iron sword
{{small|Exhibited at Meiwa Town History and Folklore Museum.}} 坂本1号墳 (明和町) 出土 須恵器.JPG|Sue pottery
{{small|Exhibited at Meiwa Town History and Folklore Museum.}} == Historic Site == === Mie Prefecture Designated Historic Site === * '''Tangible Cultural Property''': Gold-plated bronze sword pommel (archaeological artifact), 106 cm in length. Designated on March 27, 2001.{{Cite web|url=https://www.bunka.pref.mie.lg.jp/bunkazai/da/daItemDetail?mngnum=730680&pageCur=4|title=Gold-plated Bronze Sword Pommel|publisher=Mie Prefecture|language=ja}} * '''Historic Site''': Sakamoto Kofun Group. Designated on January 19, 2004, with an extension added on March 9, 2012.{{Cite web|url=https://www.bunka.pref.mie.lg.jp/bunkazai/da/daItemDetail?mngnum=730688&pageCur=5|title=Sakamoto Kofun Group|publisher=Mie Prefecture|language=ja}} == Related Facilities == * '''Meiwa Town History and Folklore Museum''': Located in Meiwa Town, this facility exhibits burial goods from the Sakamoto Kofun Group, including a reproduction of the gold-plated bronze sword pommel. == See Also == * {{ill|Saikū|en|Saikū|WD=Q150845|lt=Saikū}} == References == == External Links == {{Commonscat|Sakamoto Kofun Group (Meiwa)}} * [https://www.bunka.pref.mie.lg.jp/bunkazai/da/daItemDetail?mngnum=730688&pageCur=5 Sakamoto Kofun Group] - Mie Prefecture Official Website {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|坂本古墳群 (明和町)|version=102697946|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[ja:坂本古墳群 (明和町)]] {{Commons category|Sakamoto Kofun Group (Meiwa)}} {{wikidata link|Q88504810}} [[Category:Completion tier 3]] [[Category:Designated historic sites of Mie Prefecture]] [[Category:Gunshūfun]] [[Category:Kofun of Mie prefecture]] [[Category:Meiwa, Mie]] [[Category:Municipal History of Meiwa, Mie Prefecture]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]] [[Category:Wikidata no short description]]

Excavations were conducted on the mound and surrounding moat in 1996, and its burial chamber was examined in 1997. The zenpō-kōhō-fun mound faces south-southwest and has the following area: * Total length: 38.0 meters * Rear section: ** Length: 20.2 meters ** Width: 17.8 meters ** Height: 4.2 meters * Front section: ** Length: 11.0 meters ** Width: 15.0 meters ** Height: 2.6 meters A surrounding moat measures 2.5–4.6 meters wide and 0.6–1.0 meters deep. The burial chamber in the center of the rear section contained a log-shaped wooden coffin measuring 4.72 meters long, 0.74 meters wide, and 0.44 meters high, placed in a pit measuring 6.5 meters long, 3.5 meters wide, and 0.5–0.8 meters deep. burial goods recovered from the chamber include: * Gold-plated bronze sword pommel * Iron swords (2) * Sue pottery (yokobingata, heibingata, and hibashi types) The mound's construction is dated to the early 7th century, making it the last known zenpō-kōhō-fun in Japan. The use of a log-shaped wooden coffin and the discovery of the gold-plated bronze sword pommel, the first of its kind in Mie Prefecture, highlight its significance. 坂本1号墳 (明和町) 出土 直刀.JPG|Iron sword
{{small|Exhibited at Meiwa Town History and Folklore Museum.}} 坂本1号墳 (明和町) 出土 須恵器.JPG|Sue pottery
{{small|Exhibited at Meiwa Town History and Folklore Museum.}} == Historic Site == === Mie Prefecture Designated Historic Site === * '''Tangible Cultural Property''': Gold-plated bronze sword pommel (archaeological artifact), 106 cm in length. Designated on March 27, 2001.{{Cite web|url=https://www.bunka.pref.mie.lg.jp/bunkazai/da/daItemDetail?mngnum=730680&pageCur=4|title=Gold-plated Bronze Sword Pommel|publisher=Mie Prefecture|language=ja}} * '''Historic Site''': Sakamoto Kofun Group. Designated on January 19, 2004, with an extension added on March 9, 2012.{{Cite web|url=https://www.bunka.pref.mie.lg.jp/bunkazai/da/daItemDetail?mngnum=730688&pageCur=5|title=Sakamoto Kofun Group|publisher=Mie Prefecture|language=ja}} == Related Facilities == * '''Meiwa Town History and Folklore Museum''': Located in Meiwa Town, this facility exhibits burial goods from the Sakamoto Kofun Group, including a reproduction of the gold-plated bronze sword pommel. == See Also == * {{ill|Saikū|en|Saikū|WD=Q150845|lt=Saikū}} == References == == External Links == {{Commonscat|Sakamoto Kofun Group (Meiwa)}} * [https://www.bunka.pref.mie.lg.jp/bunkazai/da/daItemDetail?mngnum=730688&pageCur=5 Sakamoto Kofun Group] - Mie Prefecture Official Website {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|坂本古墳群 (明和町)|version=102697946|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[ja:坂本古墳群 (明和町)]] {{Commons category|Sakamoto Kofun Group (Meiwa)}} {{wikidata link|Q88504810}} [[Category:Completion tier 3]] [[Category:Designated historic sites of Mie Prefecture]] [[Category:Gunshūfun]] [[Category:Kofun of Mie prefecture]] [[Category:Meiwa, Mie]] [[Category:Municipal History of Meiwa, Mie Prefecture]] [[Category:Wikidata no short description]]

[[co:commons:Category:Sakamoto Kofun Group (Meiwa)]][[ja:坂本古墳群 (明和町)]] [[co:commons:Category:Sakamoto Kofun Group (Meiwa)]][[ja:坂本古墳群 (明和町)]] [[sn:commons:Category:Sakamoto Kofun Group (Meiwa)]][[ja:坂本古墳群 (明和町)]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q88504810]] Wikidata name: Sakamoto Kofun Cluster [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:02, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=trimming these off. the parameter works as "burial" so no need to use "Burial Facility"[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 03:35, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=added parameters Interred_individual and Mausoleum is planned to be added but I messed up in template editing[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:40, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=now we have a 'burial' and 'burial goods' parameter. So a lot of these pages might not be marked on our wiki as having unknown parameters. But specifically keeping this comment here in case of import, these parameters should be added to [[Template:infobox ancient site]] rather than deleted from this draft or article[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:02, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} Sakamoto Kofun Cluster [[File:Sakamoto Kofun Group (Meiwa), gaikan-1.jpg|thumb|300px|right|{{center|Overview of the Sakamoto Kofun Group}}]] {{location map|Japan Mie|lat_deg=34|lat_min=33|lat_sec=1.70|lon_deg=136|lon_min=37|lon_sec=5.35|map_dot_label=Sakamoto Kofun Group|position=top|width=200|mark=Red pog.svg|marksize=10|caption={{center|location of the Sakamoto Kofun Group}}}} '''Sakamoto Kofun Group''' (坂本古墳群) is a cluster of Kofuns ({{ill|Kofun|en|Kofun|WD=Q1141225|lt=Kofun}}), specifically a {{ill|gunshūfun|ja|群集墳|1=gunshūfun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/gunshūfun|13=User:Immanuelle/Gunshūfun|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11609514}}, located in {{ill|Meiwa, Mie|ja|明和町|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Meiwa, Mie|1=Meiwa, Mie|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11240358}}, Sakamoto, Mie Prefecture, Japan. Six of these mounds are Historic Site1 as Mie Prefecture Historic Sites, and the gold-plated bronze sword pommel from Mound 1 has been Historic Site1 a Tangible Cultural Property of Mie Prefecture. == Overview == [[File:Sakamoto Kofun Group (Meiwa), gaikan-2.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|View of Mounds 2–8 from Mound 1}}]] The Sakamoto Kofun Group was constructed on the western edge of a terrace on the {{ill|alluvial terrace|ja|洪積台地|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/alluvial terrace|qq=}} between the Kushida River and Miyagawa River in central Mie Prefecture. Historically, the site was referred to as "Sakamoto Hyakuhachizuka" (Sakamoto’s 108 Mounds) and included over 150 Kofuns of various types, including one {{ill|Zenpō-kōhō-fun|ja|前方後方墳|lt=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|1=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=zenpō-kōhō-fun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Zenpō-kōhō-fun|13=User:Immanuelle/zenpō-kōhō-fun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} (Mound 1), a suspected {{ill|Zenpokoenfun|en|Zenpokoenfun|WD=Q11268718|lt=Zenpokoenfun}} (Mound 5), and other round and square mounds. However, most have since been destroyed, with only six mounds (Mounds 1–6) remaining today. Investigations since 1995 have confirmed the presence of more than 30 buried mounds in addition to those still visible. The most significant is Mound 1, the only zenpō-kōhō-fun in the group and notable for being the last known Kofun of this type constructed in Japan. This mound also stands out for its burial method—a log-shaped wooden coffin (waritakegata mokkan)—and the discovery of a gold-plated bronze sword pommel. Mounds 2 and 3 are square mounds, with Mound 2 yielding earrings and Mound 3 producing iron swords during excavations. The Sakamoto Kofun Group is estimated to have been constructed during the late Kofun period, specifically in the early to mid-7th century. As one of the largest Kofun clusters in Mie Prefecture at the time, it is closely associated with the development of the Saikū (斎宮) system approximately 1 kilometer south of the site in 674 AD. This connection adds to the historical importance of the site. The six remaining mounds (Mounds 1–4, 6, and 8) were Historic Site1 as a Historic Site by Mie Prefecture in 2004. The site has been developed into the Sakamoto Kofun Park for public access. == Mound 1 == {{Infobox ancient site | name = Sakamoto Mound 1 | image =[[File:Sakamoto Kofun Group (Meiwa)-1, funkyu.jpg|280px]]
Restored mound (rear section on the left, front section on the right); File:Sakamoto Kofun Group (Meiwa), gaikan-2.jpg| location ={{ill|Meiwa, Mie|ja|明和町|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Meiwa, Mie|1=Meiwa, Mie|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11240358}}, Sakamoto (within Sakamoto Kofun Park); {{ill|Meiwa|1=Meiwa|2=ja|3=明和|WD=Q1203258|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1146161}}| coordinates ={{coordinates|34|33|1.70|N|136|37|5.35|E}}; {{Coord|34.550472222222226|N|136.61815277777777|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}}| type = {{ill|Zenpō-kōhō-fun|ja|前方後方墳|lt=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|1=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=zenpō-kōhō-fun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Zenpō-kōhō-fun|13=User:Immanuelle/zenpō-kōhō-fun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} | length = 38.0 meters | height = 4.2 meters (rear section) | burial = Waritakegata mokkan (log-shaped wooden coffin) | burial goods = Iron swords, {{ill|Sue pottery|en|Sue pottery|WD=Q7634351|lt=Sue pottery}} | built = Early 7th century | notes = Last known zenpō-kōhō-fun }} Mound 1 is the primary Kofun of the Sakamoto Kofun Group. Excavations were conducted on the mound and surrounding moat in 1996, and its burial chamber was examined in 1997. The zenpō-kōhō-fun mound faces south-southwest and has the following area: * Total length: 38.0 meters * Rear section: ** Length: 20.2 meters ** Width: 17.8 meters ** Height: 4.2 meters * Front section: ** Length: 11.0 meters ** Width: 15.0 meters ** Height: 2.6 meters A surrounding moat measures 2.5–4.6 meters wide and 0.6–1.0 meters deep. The burial chamber in the center of the rear section contained a log-shaped wooden coffin measuring 4.72 meters long, 0.74 meters wide, and 0.44 meters high, placed in a pit measuring 6.5 meters long, 3.5 meters wide, and 0.5–0.8 meters deep. burial goods recovered from the chamber include: * Gold-plated bronze sword pommel * Iron swords (2) * Sue pottery (yokobingata, heibingata, and hibashi types) The mound's construction is dated to the early 7th century, making it the last known zenpō-kōhō-fun in Japan. The use of a log-shaped wooden coffin and the discovery of the gold-plated bronze sword pommel, the first of its kind in Mie Prefecture, highlight its significance. 坂本1号墳 (明和町) 出土 直刀.JPG|Iron sword
{{small|Exhibited at Meiwa Town History and Folklore Museum.}} 坂本1号墳 (明和町) 出土 須恵器.JPG|Sue pottery
{{small|Exhibited at Meiwa Town History and Folklore Museum.}} == Historic Site == === Mie Prefecture Designated Historic Site === * '''Tangible Cultural Property''': Gold-plated bronze sword pommel (archaeological artifact), 106 cm in length. Designated on March 27, 2001.{{Cite web|url=https://www.bunka.pref.mie.lg.jp/bunkazai/da/daItemDetail?mngnum=730680&pageCur=4|title=Gold-plated Bronze Sword Pommel|publisher=Mie Prefecture|language=ja}} * '''Historic Site''': Sakamoto Kofun Group. Designated on January 19, 2004, with an extension added on March 9, 2012.{{Cite web|url=https://www.bunka.pref.mie.lg.jp/bunkazai/da/daItemDetail?mngnum=730688&pageCur=5|title=Sakamoto Kofun Group|publisher=Mie Prefecture|language=ja}} == Related Facilities == * '''Meiwa Town History and Folklore Museum''': Located in Meiwa Town, this facility exhibits burial goods from the Sakamoto Kofun Group, including a reproduction of the gold-plated bronze sword pommel. == See Also == * {{ill|Saikū|en|Saikū|WD=Q150845|lt=Saikū}} == References == == External Links == {{Commonscat|Sakamoto Kofun Group (Meiwa)}} * [https://www.bunka.pref.mie.lg.jp/bunkazai/da/daItemDetail?mngnum=730688&pageCur=5 Sakamoto Kofun Group] - Mie Prefecture Official Website {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|坂本古墳群 (明和町)|version=102697946|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[ja:坂本古墳群 (明和町)]] {{Commons category|Sakamoto Kofun Group (Meiwa)}} {{wikidata link|Q88504810}} [[Category:Completion tier 3]] [[Category:Designated historic sites of Mie Prefecture]] [[Category:Gunshūfun]] [[Category:Kofun of Mie prefecture]] [[Category:Meiwa, Mie]] [[Category:Municipal History of Meiwa, Mie Prefecture]] [[Category:Wikidata no short description]]

[[co:commons:Category:Sakamoto Kofun Group (Meiwa)]][[ja:坂本古墳群 (明和町)]] [[co:commons:Category:Sakamoto Kofun Group (Meiwa)]][[ja:坂本古墳群 (明和町)]] [[sn:commons:Category:Sakamoto Kofun Group (Meiwa)]][[ja:坂本古墳群 (明和町)]] Sakamoto Kofun Cluster [[File:Sakamoto Kofun Group (Meiwa), gaikan-1.jpg|thumb|300px|right|{{center|Overview of the Sakamoto Kofun Group}}]] {{location map|Japan Mie|lat_deg=34|lat_min=33|lat_sec=1.70|lon_deg=136|lon_min=37|lon_sec=5.35|map_dot_label=Sakamoto Kofun Group|position=top|width=200|mark=Red pog.svg|marksize=10|caption={{center|location of the Sakamoto Kofun Group}}}} '''Sakamoto Kofun Group''' (坂本古墳群) is a cluster of Kofuns ({{ill|Kofun|en|Kofun|WD=Q1141225|lt=Kofun}}), specifically a {{ill|gunshūfun|ja|群集墳|1=gunshūfun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/gunshūfun|13=User:Immanuelle/Gunshūfun|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11609514}}, located in {{ill|Meiwa, Mie|ja|明和町|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Meiwa, Mie|1=Meiwa, Mie|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11240358}}, Sakamoto, Mie Prefecture, Japan. Six of these mounds are Historic Site1 as Mie Prefecture Historic Sites, and the gold-plated bronze sword pommel from Mound 1 has been Historic Site1 a Tangible Cultural Property of Mie Prefecture. == Overview == [[File:Sakamoto Kofun Group (Meiwa), gaikan-2.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|View of Mounds 2–8 from Mound 1}}]] The Sakamoto Kofun Group was constructed on the western edge of a terrace on the {{ill|alluvial terrace|ja|洪積台地|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/alluvial terrace|qq=}} between the Kushida River and Miyagawa River in central Mie Prefecture. Historically, the site was referred to as "Sakamoto Hyakuhachizuka" (Sakamoto’s 108 Mounds) and included over 150 Kofuns of various types, including one {{ill|Zenpō-kōhō-fun|ja|前方後方墳|lt=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|1=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=zenpō-kōhō-fun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Zenpō-kōhō-fun|13=User:Immanuelle/zenpō-kōhō-fun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} (Mound 1), a suspected {{ill|Zenpokoenfun|en|Zenpokoenfun|WD=Q11268718|lt=Zenpokoenfun}} (Mound 5), and other round and square mounds. However, most have since been destroyed, with only six mounds (Mounds 1–6) remaining today. Investigations since 1995 have confirmed the presence of more than 30 buried mounds in addition to those still visible. The most significant is Mound 1, the only zenpō-kōhō-fun in the group and notable for being the last known Kofun of this type constructed in Japan. This mound also stands out for its burial method—a log-shaped wooden coffin (waritakegata mokkan)—and the discovery of a gold-plated bronze sword pommel. Mounds 2 and 3 are square mounds, with Mound 2 yielding earrings and Mound 3 producing iron swords during excavations. The Sakamoto Kofun Group is estimated to have been constructed during the late Kofun period, specifically in the early to mid-7th century. As one of the largest Kofun clusters in Mie Prefecture at the time, it is closely associated with the development of the Saikū (斎宮) system approximately 1 kilometer south of the site in 674 AD. This connection adds to the historical importance of the site. The six remaining mounds (Mounds 1–4, 6, and 8) were Historic Site1 as a Historic Site by Mie Prefecture in 2004. The site has been developed into the Sakamoto Kofun Park for public access. == Mound 1 == {{Infobox ancient site | name = Sakamoto Mound 1 | image =[[File:Sakamoto Kofun Group (Meiwa)-1, funkyu.jpg|280px]]
Restored mound (rear section on the left, front section on the right); File:Sakamoto Kofun Group (Meiwa), gaikan-2.jpg| location ={{ill|Meiwa, Mie|ja|明和町|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Meiwa, Mie|1=Meiwa, Mie|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11240358}}, Sakamoto (within Sakamoto Kofun Park); {{ill|Meiwa|1=Meiwa|2=ja|3=明和|WD=Q1203258|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1146161}}| coordinates ={{coordinates|34|33|1.70|N|136|37|5.35|E}}; {{Coord|34.550472222222226|N|136.61815277777777|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}}| type = {{ill|Zenpō-kōhō-fun|ja|前方後方墳|lt=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|1=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=zenpō-kōhō-fun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Zenpō-kōhō-fun|13=User:Immanuelle/zenpō-kōhō-fun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} | length = 38.0 meters | height = 4.2 meters (rear section) | burial = Waritakegata mokkan (log-shaped wooden coffin) | burial goods = Iron swords, {{ill|Sue pottery|en|Sue pottery|WD=Q7634351|lt=Sue pottery}} | built = Early 7th century | notes = Last known zenpō-kōhō-fun }} Mound 1 is the primary Kofun of the Sakamoto Kofun Group. Excavations were conducted on the mound and surrounding moat in 1996, and its burial chamber was examined in 1997. The zenpō-kōhō-fun mound faces south-southwest and has the following area: * Total length: 38.0 meters * Rear section: ** Length: 20.2 meters ** Width: 17.8 meters ** Height: 4.2 meters * Front section: ** Length: 11.0 meters ** Width: 15.0 meters ** Height: 2.6 meters A surrounding moat measures 2.5–4.6 meters wide and 0.6–1.0 meters deep. The burial chamber in the center of the rear section contained a log-shaped wooden coffin measuring 4.72 meters long, 0.74 meters wide, and 0.44 meters high, placed in a pit measuring 6.5 meters long, 3.5 meters wide, and 0.5–0.8 meters deep. burial goods recovered from the chamber include: * Gold-plated bronze sword pommel * Iron swords (2) * Sue pottery (yokobingata, heibingata, and hibashi types) The mound's construction is dated to the early 7th century, making it the last known zenpō-kōhō-fun in Japan. The use of a log-shaped wooden coffin and the discovery of the gold-plated bronze sword pommel, the first of its kind in Mie Prefecture, highlight its significance. 坂本1号墳 (明和町) 出土 直刀.JPG|Iron sword
{{small|Exhibited at Meiwa Town History and Folklore Museum.}} 坂本1号墳 (明和町) 出土 須恵器.JPG|Sue pottery
{{small|Exhibited at Meiwa Town History and Folklore Museum.}} == Historic Site == === Mie Prefecture Designated Historic Site === * '''Tangible Cultural Property''': Gold-plated bronze sword pommel (archaeological artifact), 106 cm in length. Designated on March 27, 2001.{{Cite web|url=https://www.bunka.pref.mie.lg.jp/bunkazai/da/daItemDetail?mngnum=730680&pageCur=4|title=Gold-plated Bronze Sword Pommel|publisher=Mie Prefecture|language=ja}} * '''Historic Site''': Sakamoto Kofun Group. Designated on January 19, 2004, with an extension added on March 9, 2012.{{Cite web|url=https://www.bunka.pref.mie.lg.jp/bunkazai/da/daItemDetail?mngnum=730688&pageCur=5|title=Sakamoto Kofun Group|publisher=Mie Prefecture|language=ja}} == Related Facilities == * '''Meiwa Town History and Folklore Museum''': Located in Meiwa Town, this facility exhibits burial goods from the Sakamoto Kofun Group, including a reproduction of the gold-plated bronze sword pommel. == See Also == * {{ill|Saikū|en|Saikū|WD=Q150845|lt=Saikū}} == References == == External Links == {{Commonscat|Sakamoto Kofun Group (Meiwa)}} * [https://www.bunka.pref.mie.lg.jp/bunkazai/da/daItemDetail?mngnum=730688&pageCur=5 Sakamoto Kofun Group] - Mie Prefecture Official Website {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|坂本古墳群 (明和町)|version=102697946|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[ja:坂本古墳群 (明和町)]] {{Commons category|Sakamoto Kofun Group (Meiwa)}} {{wikidata link|Q88504810}} [[Category:Completion tier 3]] [[Category:Designated historic sites of Mie Prefecture]] [[Category:Gunshūfun]] [[Category:Kofun of Mie prefecture]] [[Category:Meiwa, Mie]] [[Category:Municipal History of Meiwa, Mie Prefecture]] [[Category:Wikidata no short description]] [[Category:pages]]

Designated on March 27, 2001.{{Cite web|url=https://www.bunka.pref.mie.lg.jp/bunkazai/da/daItemDetail?mngnum=730680&pageCur=4|title=Gold-plated Bronze Sword Pommel|publisher=Mie Prefecture|language=ja}} * '''Historic Site''': Sakamoto Kofun Group. Designated on January 19, 2004, with an extension added on March 9, 2012.{{Cite web|url=https://www.bunka.pref.mie.lg.jp/bunkazai/da/daItemDetail?mngnum=730688&pageCur=5|title=Sakamoto Kofun Group|publisher=Mie Prefecture|language=ja}} == Related Facilities == * '''Meiwa Town History and Folklore Museum''': Located in Meiwa Town, this facility exhibits burial goods from the Sakamoto Kofun Group, including a reproduction of the gold-plated bronze sword pommel. == See Also == * {{ill|Saikū|en|Saikū|WD=Q150845|lt=Saikū|1=Saikū}} == References == == External Links == {{Commonscat|Sakamoto Kofun Group (Meiwa)}} * [https://www.bunka.pref.mie.lg.jp/bunkazai/da/daItemDetail?mngnum=730688&pageCur=5 Sakamoto Kofun Group] - Mie Prefecture Official Website {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|坂本古墳群 (明和町)|version=102697946|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[ja:坂本古墳群 (明和町)]] {{Commons category|Sakamoto Kofun Group (Meiwa)}} {{wikidata link|Q88504810}} [[Category:Completion tier 3]] [[Category:Designated historic sites of Mie Prefecture]] [[Category:Gunshūfun]] [[Category:Kofun of Mie prefecture]] [[Category:Meiwa, Mie]] [[Category:Municipal History of Meiwa, Mie Prefecture]] [[Category:Wikidata no short description]] [[Category:pages]]

[[co:commons:Category:Sakamoto Kofun Group (Meiwa)]][[ja:坂本古墳群 (明和町)]] [[co:commons:Category:Sakamoto Kofun Group (Meiwa)]][[ja:坂本古墳群 (明和町)]] [[Category:sn_crud|TESTcommons:Category:Sakamoto Kofun Group (Meiwa)]][[ja:坂本古墳群 (明和町)]] Sakamoto Kofun Cluster [[File:Sakamoto Kofun Group (Meiwa), gaikan-1.jpg|thumb|300px|right|{{center|Overview of the Sakamoto Kofun Group}}]] {{location map|Japan Mie|lat_deg=34|lat_min=33|lat_sec=1.70|lon_deg=136|lon_min=37|lon_sec=5.35|map_dot_label=Sakamoto Kofun Group|position=top|width=200|mark=Red pog.svg|marksize=10|caption={{center|location of the Sakamoto Kofun Group}}}} '''Sakamoto Kofun Group''' (坂本古墳群) is a cluster of Kofuns ({{ill|Kofun|en|Kofun|WD=Q1141225|lt=Kofun|1=Kofun}}), specifically a {{ill|gunshūfun|ja|群集墳|1=Gunshūfun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/gunshūfun|13=User:Immanuelle/Gunshūfun|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11609514}}, located in {{ill|Meiwa, Mie|ja|明和町|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Meiwa, Mie|1=Q11240358|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11240358}}, Sakamoto, Mie Prefecture, Japan. Six of these mounds are Historic Site1 as Mie Prefecture Historic Sites, and the gold-plated bronze sword pommel from Mound 1 has been Historic Site1 a Tangible Cultural Property of Mie Prefecture. == Overview == [[File:Sakamoto Kofun Group (Meiwa), gaikan-2.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|View of Mounds 2–8 from Mound 1}}]] The Sakamoto Kofun Group was constructed on the western edge of a terrace on the {{ill|alluvial terrace|ja|洪積台地|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/alluvial terrace|qq=}} between the Kushida River and Miyagawa River in central Mie Prefecture. Historically, the site was referred to as "Sakamoto Hyakuhachizuka" (Sakamoto’s 108 Mounds) and included over 150 Kofuns of various types, including one {{ill|Zenpō-kōhō-fun|ja|前方後方墳|lt=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|1=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=zenpō-kōhō-fun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Zenpō-kōhō-fun|13=User:Immanuelle/zenpō-kōhō-fun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} (Mound 1), a suspected {{ill|Zenpokoenfun|en|Zenpokoenfun|WD=Q11268718|lt=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpōkōenfun}} (Mound 5), and other round and square mounds. However, most have since been destroyed, with only six mounds (Mounds 1–6) remaining today. Investigations since 1995 have confirmed the presence of more than 30 buried mounds in addition to those still visible. The most significant is Mound 1, the only zenpō-kōhō-fun in the group and notable for being the last known Kofun of this type constructed in Japan. This mound also stands out for its burial method—a log-shaped wooden coffin (waritakegata mokkan)—and the discovery of a gold-plated bronze sword pommel. Mounds 2 and 3 are square mounds, with Mound 2 yielding earrings and Mound 3 producing iron swords during excavations. The Sakamoto Kofun Group is estimated to have been constructed during the late Kofun period, specifically in the early to mid-7th century. As one of the largest Kofun clusters in Mie Prefecture at the time, it is closely associated with the development of the Saikū (斎宮) system approximately 1 kilometer south of the site in 674 AD. This connection adds to the historical importance of the site. The six remaining mounds (Mounds 1–4, 6, and 8) were Historic Site1 as a Historic Site by Mie Prefecture in 2004. The site has been developed into the Sakamoto Kofun Park for public access. == Mound 1 == {{Infobox ancient site | name = Sakamoto Mound 1 | image =[[File:Sakamoto Kofun Group (Meiwa)-1, funkyu.jpg|280px]]
Restored mound (rear section on the left, front section on the right); File:Sakamoto Kofun Group (Meiwa), gaikan-2.jpg| location ={{ill|Meiwa, Mie|ja|明和町|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Meiwa, Mie|1=Q11240358|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11240358}}, Sakamoto (within Sakamoto Kofun Park); {{ill|Meiwa|1=Meiwa, Mie|2=ja|3=明和|WD=Q1203258|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1146161}}| coordinates ={{coordinates|34|33|1.70|N|136|37|5.35|E}}; {{Coord|34.550472222222226|N|136.61815277777777|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}}| type = {{ill|Zenpō-kōhō-fun|ja|前方後方墳|lt=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|1=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=zenpō-kōhō-fun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Zenpō-kōhō-fun|13=User:Immanuelle/zenpō-kōhō-fun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} | length = 38.0 meters | height = 4.2 meters (rear section) | burial = Waritakegata mokkan (log-shaped wooden coffin) | burial goods = Iron swords, {{ill|Sue pottery|en|Sue pottery|WD=Q7634351|lt=Sue pottery|1=Sue pottery}} | built = Early 7th century | notes = Last known zenpō-kōhō-fun }} Mound 1 is the primary Kofun of the Sakamoto Kofun Group. Excavations were conducted on the mound and surrounding moat in 1996, and its burial chamber was examined in 1997. The zenpō-kōhō-fun mound faces south-southwest and has the following area: * Total length: 38.0 meters * Rear section: ** Length: 20.2 meters ** Width: 17.8 meters ** Height: 4.2 meters * Front section: ** Length: 11.0 meters ** Width: 15.0 meters ** Height: 2.6 meters A surrounding moat measures 2.5–4.6 meters wide and 0.6–1.0 meters deep. The burial chamber in the center of the rear section contained a log-shaped wooden coffin measuring 4.72 meters long, 0.74 meters wide, and 0.44 meters high, placed in a pit measuring 6.5 meters long, 3.5 meters wide, and 0.5–0.8 meters deep. burial goods recovered from the chamber include: * Gold-plated bronze sword pommel * Iron swords (2) * Sue pottery (yokobingata, heibingata, and hibashi types) The mound's construction is dated to the early 7th century, making it the last known zenpō-kōhō-fun in Japan. The use of a log-shaped wooden coffin and the discovery of the gold-plated bronze sword pommel, the first of its kind in Mie Prefecture, highlight its significance. 坂本1号墳 (明和町) 出土 直刀.JPG|Iron sword
{{small|Exhibited at Meiwa Town History and Folklore Museum.}} 坂本1号墳 (明和町) 出土 須恵器.JPG|Sue pottery
{{small|Exhibited at Meiwa Town History and Folklore Museum.}} == Historic Site == === Mie Prefecture Designated Historic Site === * '''Tangible Cultural Property''': Gold-plated bronze sword pommel (archaeological artifact), 106 cm in length. Designated on March 27, 2001.{{Cite web|url=https://www.bunka.pref.mie.lg.jp/bunkazai/da/daItemDetail?mngnum=730680&pageCur=4|title=Gold-plated Bronze Sword Pommel|publisher=Mie Prefecture|language=ja}} * '''Historic Site''': Sakamoto Kofun Group. Designated on January 19, 2004, with an extension added on March 9, 2012.{{Cite web|url=https://www.bunka.pref.mie.lg.jp/bunkazai/da/daItemDetail?mngnum=730688&pageCur=5|title=Sakamoto Kofun Group|publisher=Mie Prefecture|language=ja}} == Related Facilities == * '''Meiwa Town History and Folklore Museum''': Located in Meiwa Town, this facility exhibits burial goods from the Sakamoto Kofun Group, including a reproduction of the gold-plated bronze sword pommel. == See Also == * {{ill|Saikū|en|Saikū|WD=Q150845|lt=Saikū|1=Saikū}} == References == == External Links == {{Commonscat|Sakamoto Kofun Group (Meiwa)}} * [https://www.bunka.pref.mie.lg.jp/bunkazai/da/daItemDetail?mngnum=730688&pageCur=5 Sakamoto Kofun Group] - Mie Prefecture Official Website {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|坂本古墳群 (明和町)|version=102697946|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[ja:坂本古墳群 (明和町)]] {{Commons category|Sakamoto Kofun Group (Meiwa)}} {{wikidata link|Q88504810}} [[Category:Designated historic sites of Mie Prefecture]] [[Category:Gunshūfun]] [[Category:Kofun of Mie prefecture]] [[Category:Meiwa, Mie]] [[Category:Municipal History of Meiwa, Mie Prefecture]] [[Category:Wikidata no short description]] [[Category:pages]]

Excavations were conducted on the mound and surrounding moat in 1996, and its burial chamber was examined in 1997. The zenpō-kōhō-fun mound faces south-southwest and has the following area: * Total length: 38.0 meters * Rear section: ** Length: 20.2 meters ** Width: 17.8 meters ** Height: 4.2 meters * Front section: ** Length: 11.0 meters ** Width: 15.0 meters ** Height: 2.6 meters A surrounding moat measures 2.5–4.6 meters wide and 0.6–1.0 meters deep. The burial chamber in the center of the rear section contained a log-shaped wooden coffin measuring 4.72 meters long, 0.74 meters wide, and 0.44 meters high, placed in a pit measuring 6.5 meters long, 3.5 meters wide, and 0.5–0.8 meters deep. burial goods recovered from the chamber include: * Gold-plated bronze sword pommel * Iron swords (2) * Sue pottery (yokobingata, heibingata, and hibashi types) The mound's construction is dated to the early 7th century, making it the last known zenpō-kōhō-fun in Japan. The use of a log-shaped wooden coffin and the discovery of the gold-plated bronze sword pommel, the first of its kind in Mie Prefecture, highlight its significance. 坂本1号墳 (明和町) 出土 直刀.JPG|Iron sword
{{small|Exhibited at Meiwa Town History and Folklore Museum.}} 坂本1号墳 (明和町) 出土 須恵器.JPG|Sue pottery
{{small|Exhibited at Meiwa Town History and Folklore Museum.}} == Historic Site == === Mie Prefecture Designated Historic Site === * '''Tangible Cultural Property''': Gold-plated bronze sword pommel (archaeological artifact), 106 cm in length. Designated on March 27, 2001.{{Cite web|url=https://www.bunka.pref.mie.lg.jp/bunkazai/da/daItemDetail?mngnum=730680&pageCur=4|title=Gold-plated Bronze Sword Pommel|publisher=Mie Prefecture|language=ja}} * '''Historic Site''': Sakamoto Kofun Group. Designated on January 19, 2004, with an extension added on March 9, 2012.{{Cite web|url=https://www.bunka.pref.mie.lg.jp/bunkazai/da/daItemDetail?mngnum=730688&pageCur=5|title=Sakamoto Kofun Group|publisher=Mie Prefecture|language=ja}} == Related Facilities == * '''Meiwa Town History and Folklore Museum''': Located in Meiwa Town, this facility exhibits burial goods from the Sakamoto Kofun Group, including a reproduction of the gold-plated bronze sword pommel. == See Also == * {{ill|Saikū|en|Saikū|WD=Q150845|lt=Saikū|1=Saikū}} == References == == External Links == {{Commonscat|Sakamoto Kofun Group (Meiwa)}} * [https://www.bunka.pref.mie.lg.jp/bunkazai/da/daItemDetail?mngnum=730688&pageCur=5 Sakamoto Kofun Group] - Mie Prefecture Official Website {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|坂本古墳群 (明和町)|version=102697946|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[ja:坂本古墳群 (明和町)]] {{Commons category|Sakamoto Kofun Group (Meiwa)}} {{wikidata link|Q88504810}} [[Category:Designated historic sites of Mie Prefecture]] [[Category:Gunshūfun]] [[Category:Kofun of Mie prefecture]] [[Category:Meiwa, Mie]] [[Category:Municipal History of Meiwa, Mie Prefecture]] [[Category:Wikidata no short description]]
